evals.prompt: In my Next.js App Router app I have components/Hero.tsx (a client component) with an <h1>. Animate the headline so each line wipes up into view on mount. Make sure it only targets this heading, not headings elsewhere on the page.
evals.expected_output: A client component using useGSAP with a scoped container ref, animating the heading lines (SplitText with type/mask lines, or per-line spans) on mount with a stagger, cleaning up automatically, and honoring prefers-reduced-motion.
assertions: Uses the useGSAP hook from @gsap/react (not a bare useEffect) for setup and cleanup
Passes a scope (a ref to the component root) so selectors do not match elements outside the component
Keeps the file a client component ('use client') and does not run GSAP during SSR
Animates the lines with a stagger (e.g. SplitText lines or per-line wrappers) using a from/timeline reveal
Provides a prefers-reduced-motion fallback (e.g. gsap.matchMedia) that shows the text without motion

evals.prompt: Add a pinned section to my landing page that scrubs through three cards as the user scrolls. It's React with TypeScript.
evals.expected_output: A client component using useGSAP that builds a timeline with a ScrollTrigger that pins the section and scrubs it to scroll, using ease none inside scrubbed tweens, with automatic cleanup and a reduced-motion path that avoids pinning.
assertions: Registers and uses ScrollTrigger with pin: true and scrub on the trigger
Drives a timeline (or single tween) with the ScrollTrigger rather than putting scrollTrigger on nested child tweens
Uses ease: 'none' for scrubbed tweens so motion tracks scroll linearly
Wraps setup in useGSAP for cleanup, or otherwise kills the ScrollTrigger on unmount
Does not combine scrub with toggleActions
Addresses prefers-reduced-motion (e.g. no pin / static layout when reduce is set)

evals.prompt: Build a horizontal-scrolling gallery: a wide row of panels that moves sideways while I scroll down the page. Next.js client component.
evals.expected_output: A client component that pins a viewport and translates the track on the x axis tied to scroll, computing the distance from scrollWidth, using a function-based end and invalidateOnRefresh, with cleanup and reduced-motion fallback.
assertions: Pins a viewport and animates the track horizontally (x / xPercent) with scrub and ease: 'none'
Computes scroll distance from the track width (e.g. scrollWidth - innerWidth) and uses a function-based end with invalidateOnRefresh: true
Uses useGSAP with a scope for setup and automatic cleanup
Marks the component 'use client'
Provides a non-animated/normal-layout fallback for prefers-reduced-motion

evals.prompt: Make a magnetic button in React that smoothly follows the cursor while hovering and springs back when the pointer leaves.
evals.expected_output: A client component using useGSAP and contextSafe handlers; cursor tracking via gsap.quickTo (or a reused tween) for performance, an elastic/power ease return, and a reduced-motion guard.
assertions: Uses gsap.quickTo (or a single reused tween) for the per-pointer-move update rather than creating a new gsap.to on every mousemove
Wraps event handlers in contextSafe (from useGSAP) so they are cleaned up
Animates with transform aliases (x/y) not layout properties
Resets the button on mouse leave with an ease (e.g. elastic/power)
Guards the effect for prefers-reduced-motion

evals.prompt: I'm getting SSR/hydration errors when I use GSAP in my Next.js App Router project. How should I set it up so it works?
evals.expected_output: Guidance to run GSAP client-side only: mark components 'use client', use useGSAP (SSR-safe) instead of useEffect/useLayoutEffect at top level, register plugins once at a client boundary, and never call gsap/ScrollTrigger during server render.
assertions: Explains GSAP must run client-side only and components need 'use client'
Recommends the useGSAP hook (SSR-safe via isomorphic layout effect) rather than running GSAP during render/SSR
Shows registering plugins once (e.g. a lib/gsap.ts module) at a client boundary
Notes installing both gsap and @gsap/react

evals.prompt: Animate an SVG logo in React: draw the outline stroke on, then morph the shape into a different icon.
evals.expected_output: A client component registering DrawSVGPlugin and MorphSVGPlugin, using a timeline to draw the stroke (drawSVG) then morph (morphSVG) into a target path, with cleanup and reduced-motion fallback to the final state.
assertions: Registers DrawSVGPlugin and MorphSVGPlugin (imported from the gsap package) before use
Uses drawSVG to reveal the stroke and morphSVG to morph between shapes, sequenced on a timeline
Notes these plugins are free (no Club GreenSock / trial / license needed)
Uses useGSAP for cleanup and renders the final state when prefers-reduced-motion is set

evals.prompt: Reveal a grid of cards with a stagger as they scroll into view, but I don't want a separate ScrollTrigger created for every single card.
evals.expected_output: Use ScrollTrigger.batch to group elements entering together and stagger them in batches, with onEnter/onLeaveBack, inside useGSAP, plus a reduced-motion fallback that shows all cards.
assertions: Uses ScrollTrigger.batch to group the cards instead of one ScrollTrigger per card
Staggers the batched elements in onEnter and uses overwrite to avoid conflicts
Sets the initial hidden state and uses useGSAP for cleanup
Provides a prefers-reduced-motion fallback that reveals all cards without animation

evals.prompt: My GSAP ScrollTrigger animations end up at the wrong scroll positions after images load and when I navigate between routes in my Next.js app. How do I fix this?
evals.expected_output: Call ScrollTrigger.refresh() after layout-affecting content (images/fonts/async) settles; on App Router navigation kill existing ScrollTriggers and refresh; prefer useGSAP keyed on pathname so triggers are recreated/cleaned per route.
assertions: Recommends ScrollTrigger.refresh() after images/fonts/async content change the layout
Explains ScrollTrigger does not auto-clean on Next.js route changes and triggers must be killed/recreated on navigation
Uses useGSAP (e.g. keyed on usePathname) or an equivalent cleanup so triggers are reverted per route
Mentions function-based start/end or invalidateOnRefresh for responsive recalculation
